meta.name: kubernetes-deployment-best-practices
meta.title: Kubernetes Deployment Best Practices Skill
meta.description: Comprehensive best practices for deploying and managing applications on Kubernetes. Covers Pods, Deployments, Services, Ingress, ConfigMaps, Secrets, health checks, resource limits, scaling, and secur

original_definition.skill_description: Comprehensive best practices for deploying and managing applications on Kubernetes. Covers Pods, Deployments, Services, Ingress, ConfigMaps, Secrets, health checks, resource limits, scaling, and secur

actions.name: Principle: The smallest deployable unit in Kubernetes. Repre
actions.description: Principle: The smallest deployable unit in Kubernetes. Represents a single instance of a running process in your cluster.

actions.name: Design Pods to run a single primary container (or tightly co
actions.description: Design Pods to run a single primary container (or tightly coupled sidecars).

actions.name: Principle: An abstract way to expose an application running
actions.description: Principle: An abstract way to expose an application running on a set of Pods as a network service.

actions.name: Choose `type` based on exposure needs (ClusterIP, NodePort,
actions.description: Choose `type` based on exposure needs (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer, ExternalName).

actions.name: Pro Tip: Use `ClusterIP` for internal services, `LoadBalance
actions.description: Pro Tip: Use `ClusterIP` for internal services, `LoadBalancer` for internet-facing applications in cloud environments.

actions.name: Configure Ingress resources for external access when using a
actions.description: Configure Ingress resources for external access when using a web application.

actions.name: Store secrets encrypted at rest in etcd (if your cluster is
actions.description: Store secrets encrypted at rest in etcd (if your cluster is configured for it).

actions.name: Pro Tip: For production, integrate with external secret mana
actions.description: Pro Tip: For production, integrate with external secret managers (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault) using external Secrets

actions.name: Principle: Determines if a container is still running. If it
actions.description: Principle: Determines if a container is still running. If it fails, Kubernetes restarts the container.

actions.name: Principle: Determines if a container is ready to serve traff
actions.description: Principle: Determines if a container is ready to serve traffic. If it fails, Kubernetes removes the Pod from Service load balancers.

actions.name: Pro Tip: Use readiness probes to gracefully remove Pods duri
actions.description: Pro Tip: Use readiness probes to gracefully remove Pods during startup or temporary outages.

rules.description: As GitHub Copilot, you are an expert in Kubernetes deployments, with deep knowledge of best practices for running applications reliably, securely, and
Define `resources` (requests/limits) for CPU and memory to prevent resource exhaustion.
Pro Tip: Avoid deploying Pods directly; use higher-level controllers like Deployments or StatefulSets.
Principle: Manages a set of identical Pods and ensures they are running. Handles rolling updates and rollbacks.
Ensure `selector` matches Pod labels for proper routing.
Caution: ConfigMaps are not encrypted at rest. Do NOT store sensitive data here.
Guidance for Copilot: Implement an HTTP, TCP, or command-based liveness probe to ensure the application is active.
Principle: Define CPU and memory requests/limits for every container.
Limits: Hard maximum resources (prevents noisy neighbors and resource exhaustion).

directives.description: Use Services to provide stable network identity to Pods.
Principle: Manages external access to services in a cluster, typically HTTP/HTTPS routes from outside the cluster to services within.
Use Ingress to consolidate routing rules and manage TLS termination.
Principle: Store non-sensitive configuration data as key-value pairs.
Use ConfigMaps for application configuration, environment variables, or command-line arguments.
Mount ConfigMaps as files in Pods or inject as environment variables.
Use Kubernetes Secrets for API keys, passwords, database credentials, TLS certificates.
Mount Secrets as volumes (files) or inject as environment variables (use caution with env vars).
